title: Symmetric successive over-relaxation
summary:
In applied mathematics, symmetric successive over-relaxation (SSOR), is a preconditioner.
If the original matrix can be split into diagonal, lower and upper triangular as 
  
    
      
        A
        =
        D
        +
        L
        +
        
          L
          
            
              T
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle A=D+L+L^{\mathsf {T}}}
  
 then the SSOR preconditioner matrix is defined as

  
    
      
        M
        =
        (
        D
        +
        L
        )
        
          D
          
            −
            1
          
        
        (
        D
        +
        L
        
          )
          
            
              T
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle M=(D+L)D^{-1}(D+L)^{\mathsf {T}}}
  

It can also be parametrised by 
  
    
      
        ω
      
    
    {\displaystyle \omega }
  
 as follows.

  
    
      
        M
        (
        ω
        )
        =
        
          
            ω
            
              2
              −
              ω
            
          
        
        
          (
          
            
              
                1
                ω
              
            
            D
            +
            L
          
          )
        
        
          D
          
            −
            1
          
        
        
          
            (
            
              
                
                  1
                  ω
                
              
              D
              +
              L
            
            )
          
          
            
              T
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle M(\omega )={\omega  \over {2-\omega }}\left({1 \over \omega }D+L\right)D^{-1}\left({1 \over \omega }D+L\right)^{\mathsf {T}}}
